channel.title: KiddieDaily — News for Families
channel.description: Daily kid-friendly news with bias indicators and fact checks. Updated every morning.

item.title: Crutchfield’s Fourth of July headphone and earbud sale is all fireworks no fizzle
item.description: Crutchfield’s July 4 sale slashes Bose, Sennheiser, JBL, Marshall, and more—grab headphone and earbud deals before they vanish. The post Crutchfield’s Fourth of July headphone and earbud sale is — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Have scientists really made a living cell from scratch? Not quite
item.description: A prototype cell partly capable of replicating itself has been created using 36 existing bacterial genes, but it's not really a living organism – yet — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Newly formed Tropical Storm Douglas poses no threat to land, hurricane center says
item.description: Douglas was located about 1,220 miles west-southwest of the southern tip of Mexico's Baja California, the National Hurricane Center said. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Don't expect trackers to save your stolen car, experts say
item.description: Kia told the BBC UK law prevented its location tracking function being used to live track vehicles. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Apple butter, sightseeing and puppets: How UK is marking US independence milestone
item.description: As America turns 250 years old, people across the pond are running events of their own to mark the big breakup. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: As Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce prepare to tie the knot, we look back at their relationship
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to celebrate their wedding with a party at New York's Madison Square Garden this weekend. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: The 5 Types of Aliens People Most Frequently Say They See
item.description: From the Greys to the Nordics, these aliens appear frequently across abduction stories and science fiction tales. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Diesel sees biggest monthly fall in 26 years. What's happening to fuel prices?
item.description: When the US-Israel war with Iran began, fuel costs jumped, but they are now plummeting as hope of lasting peace grows. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: 'Kane to the rescue' and 'defence black hole triples'
item.description: England Captain Harry Kane's World Cup goals against DR Congo dominate Thursday's front pages. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: US judge sides with NAACP over proposed mail-in ballot restrictions
item.description: President Trump has sought to limit mail-in voting and has ordered his administration to impose limits on the practice. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Iran football team welcomed home after World Cup exit
item.description: Iran’s football team has landed back in Tehran after their World Cup — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Belgium stage 3–2 comeback win over Senegal to enter World Cup last 16
item.description: Tielemans's penalty late into stoppage time of extra time capped Belgium's comeback from 2-0 down in Seattle. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: ICE surges enforcement, makes 10,000 arrests in five days amid Supreme Court birthright citizenship decision
item.description: ICE made over 10,000 arrests in five days as the Supreme Court upheld birthright citizenship under the Fourteenth Amendment, rejecting Trump bid. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Fox News Poll: A close Senate contest is brewing in Iowa
item.description: Democrat Josh Turek holds a 4-point lead over Republican Ashley Hinson in the 2026 Iowa Senate race as Donald Trump favorability drops statewide. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Scientists Create Tiny “Mini Livers” That Could One Day Replace Liver Transplants
item.description: Engineered tissue grafts could help perform key liver functions and benefit thousands of people living with liver failure. The liver is one of the body’s hardest-working organs, carrying out hun — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: What the Supreme Court did on the final day of its term
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the right of children born on U.S. soil to automatic American citizenship. In so doing, the court rejected President Trump's most aggressive attempt to limit immigration. — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Editor's note: NPR retracts Justice Samuel Alito story
item.description: 2 outlets covering this story — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Serena Williams loses singles match in opening round at Wimbledon
item.description: Williams played two doubles matches just before Wimbledon to announce her comeback, but she hadn't played a singles match since the 2022 U.S. Open. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: US women Olympians react to historic Supreme Court ruling on the protection of women's sports
item.description: Olympic gold medalists celebrate the Supreme Court ruling upholding state laws that ban transgender athletes from competing in women's sports. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: 5 Things Princess Diana Loved That Might Surprise You
item.description: From one specific cocktail to astrology, here are five things you might not know Princess Diana was fond of. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.
